summary: This open PR was a small build-configuration/linkage update: it edited only configure.py to move several existing object files from NonMatching to Matching, thereby linking them into the matching build. The decomp.dev bot reported a sizeable progress increase for GALE01: linked code rose to 43.26% (+0.90%, +35120 bytes) and linked data to 35.06% (+0.07%, +824 bytes), with no reported changes.
what_changed: In configure.py, Object(NonMatching, "melee/pl/plattack.c") was changed to Object(Matching, "melee/pl/plattack.c").
In configure.py, Object(NonMatching, "melee/pl/plbonus.c") was changed to Object(Matching, "melee/pl/plbonus.c").
In configure.py, Object(NonMatching, "melee/ft/ftchangeparam.c") was changed to Object(Matching, "melee/ft/ftchangeparam.c").
In configure.py, Object(NonMatching, "melee/ft/chara/ftPp_SpecialS.c") was changed to Object(Matching, "melee/ft/chara/ftPp_SpecialS.c").
In configure.py, Object(NonMatching, "sysdolphin/baselib/bytecode.c") was changed to Object(Matching, "sysdolphin/baselib/bytecode.c").

The NonMatching entry for "melee/ft/ft_3C61.c" was removed from the object list near the ftCommon Kirby-capture entries. The PR slice does not explain whether this was because the file became redundant, relocated, or otherwise no longer needed in that position.
No C source or header files were changed in this slice; the functional impact is through the build object's Matching/NonMatching classification.
smart_moves: Kept the PR minimal by touching only configure.py, which is appropriate when the source files already match and only need to be linked as Matching objects.
Batched several 'free' object promotions in one small configure change, immediately converting existing matching work into measurable linked-code and linked-data progress.
Relied on the project’s existing Object(Matching, path) convention rather than changing source to force progress.

The decomp.dev report provided quick validation that the linkage-only change increased linked code/data and reported 'No changes', suggesting no behavioral diff was introduced by this configuration update.
decomp_lessons: When a decompiled C file is already matching but still listed as NonMatching in configure.py, progress can be unlocked by flipping only the Object status to Matching.
Linkage PRs should be evidence-backed by progress reports; here the bot quantified the exact GALE01 linked-code and linked-data gains.

A configure-only PR is a low-noise way to land completed matching work, but removals such as the deleted ft_3C61.c entry should ideally be explained in the PR body or comments if the reason is not obvious from the diff.
Future sweeps can search configure.py for stale Object(NonMatching, ...) entries whose corresponding source has become matchable, then promote them without unrelated source churn.
naming_conventions: Build object entries use Object(Matching, "path/to/file.c") and Object(NonMatching, "path/to/file.c") in configure.py.
Player subsystem files in this slice use lowercase pl-prefixed names such as melee/pl/plattack.c, melee/pl/plbonus.c, melee/pl/plbonuslib.c, and melee/pl/pl_040D.c.
Fighter common files in this area include ftCo_ CamelCase action names such as ftCo_CaptureKirby.c and ftCo_ItemParasolFall.c.
Ice Climbers/Popo action files use ftPp_ prefixes, including melee/ft/chara/ftPopo/ftPp_SpecialS.c and the promoted melee/ft/chara/ftPp_SpecialS.c path as shown in the diff.
sysdolphin baselib object entries use lowercase module names such as shadow.c, archive.c, random.c, bytecode.c, class.c, hash.c, and texp.c.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No assembly edits or source-level matching tactics are visible in the PR slice.
The matching tactic evidenced here is configuration promotion: convert known-good decompiled objects from NonMatching to Matching once they are safe to link.
Use decomp.dev linked-code/data deltas as a sanity check after configure.py object-status changes.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the PR slice.
The only comment was from decomp-dev[bot]: 'Report for GALE01 (8b90263 - 9a8eaa8)' with linked code 43.26% (+0.90%, +35120 bytes), linked data 35.06% (+0.07%, +824 bytes), and 'No changes'.
follow_up_queries: Find other configure.py entries still marked Object(NonMatching, ...) whose source files may now be matchable.
Check history around melee/ft/ft_3C61.c to understand why its NonMatching object entry was removed instead of changed to Matching.
Verify whether melee/ft/chara/ftPp_SpecialS.c is intentionally outside the ftPopo directory or whether the path reflects an existing project layout convention.
Look for subsequent PRs or reviews that merged or adjusted this open linkage PR.
